headline: PNB's ASB Returns Drop to 4.25% — Lowest in 18 Years
context: Permodalan Nasional Berhad declared a 4.25% distribution for Amanah Saham Bumiputera in 2025, the lowest since 2007, affecting 10.4 million unitholders who rely on ASB as their primary savings vehicle.

cards.big: 4.25% ASB return affects 10.4 million Bumiputera savers — many borrowed via ASB financing at 4.5% interest
cards.sub: For the first time, hundreds of thousands of ASB loan holders are paying more in interest than they earn

cards.big: RM320 billion ASB fund is 68% invested in Malaysian equities — Bursa's 2.1% return dragged overall performance
cards.sub: Domestic equity concentration means ASB returns mirror Bursa — which has underperformed regional peers for 5 years

cards.big: 2.8 million ASB financing borrowers owe RM92 billion in bank loans secured against their units
cards.sub: Banks earned RM4.1 billion in ASB loan interest in 2025 — often from B40 borrowers who can least afford negative carry

cards.big: PNB's investment committee composition and stock selection criteria remain classified from unitholders
cards.sub: 10.4 million unitholders have no voting rights, no AGM, and no mechanism to influence RM320 billion in decisions

cards.big: ASB was meant to build Bumiputera wealth — ASB financing turned it into a way for banks to extract it
Should BNM ban ASB financing products when distribution rates fall below the loan interest rate?
